description:
You are working as a performance-tuning engineer and have just finished running three
  micro-benchmarks.  
  Their raw timing logs have already been saved (one log per benchmark) in the directory

      /home/user/samples

  and their filenames are:

      webserver.log
      cache.log
      db.log

  Each of those files contains exactly one line, in the form

      exec_time_ms=<NUMBER>

  where <NUMBER> is the total execution time of that benchmark in milliseconds  
  (e.g. “exec_time_ms=123”).

  You now need to prepare a **quick report** for your team that shows which benchmark
  ran the fastest and preserves its full log for later inspection.

  1. Create the directory  

         /home/user/profiling/reports  

     (create parent directories if they don’t already exist).

  2. Determine which of the three benchmarks recorded the **lowest** exec_time_ms
     value.

  3. Copy that benchmark’s original log file into  

         /home/user/profiling/reports/best_app.log

     so that best_app.log is an exact byte-for-byte duplicate of the original log
     (do **not** rename the original).

  4. In the same /home/user/profiling/reports directory, create a plain-text file
     named summary.log that contains **exactly** the two lines shown below, with the
     correct benchmark name and timing value substituted:

         FASTEST_APP: <benchmark_name>
         EXEC_TIME_MS: <number>

     Formatting rules that the automated checker will enforce:
       • The keys FASTEST_APP and EXEC_TIME_MS must be in uppercase as shown.  
       • Exactly one colon ‘:’ and one space follow each key.  
       • No leading or trailing spaces on either line.  
       • Only a single newline separates the two lines (i.e. the file ends with a
         newline after the second line).  
       • No extra blank lines anywhere in the file.

  When you are finished, the /home/user/profiling/reports directory should contain
    • best_app.log   (the winning log, perfectly copied)  
    • summary.log    (the two-line summary described above)

  The automated tests will:
    • Verify the directory exists.  
    • Validate that best_app.log is a byte-for-byte copy of the correct original
      source log.  
    • Parse summary.log and confirm that the benchmark name and execution-time
      number match the chosen log file’s contents and follow the exact format.
truth:
<!-- Pre-existing files before the agent begins -->
  <initial_files>
    <file path="/home/user/samples/webserver.log">exec_time_ms=250\n</file>
    <file path="/home/user/samples/cache.log">exec_time_ms=75\n</file>
    <file path="/home/user/samples/db.log">exec_time_ms=184\n</file>
  </initial_files>

  <!-- Expected final state after successful completion -->
  <expected_files>
    <!-- Directory must exist -->
    <dir path="/home/user/profiling/reports"/>

    <!-- best_app.log must be an exact copy of cache.log -->
    <file path="/home/user/profiling/reports/best_app.log"
          checksum="sha256">d4a7d3624e267af7b1e6bc3b145c4445eecf9817cf7296147401c7cafdbee684</file>
    <!-- Above checksum is sha256 of "exec_time_ms=75\n" -->

    <!-- summary.log must contain exactly the two lines below with a
         single trailing newline at the end of the file -->
    <file path="/home/user/profiling/reports/summary.log">FASTEST_APP: cache\nEXEC_TIME_MS: 75\n</file>
  </expected_files>
name: task_000000_f0d61f04
